title: SIP integration documents event names that contradict the notification event contract

evidence.quote: The frontend listens for three primary Socket.IO events for real-time updates.

reasoning: The SIP integration guide tells consumers to listen for namespaced Socket.IO events such as 'call:incoming', 'call:answered', 'call:ended', and 'call:missed'. The Notifications section for the same docs feature describes the frontend's call-related event contract as 'call-event' and 'incoming-call'. This internal contradiction means an integrator following the SIP guide will subscribe to event names that the rest of the docs do not recognize, causing real-time call updates to be missed.
recommendation: Align SIPIntegration with the canonical event names and payload shape used elsewhere in the docs, or explicitly explain if these are separate low-level server events. Prefer documenting 'call-event' for status updates and 'incoming-call' for popups if those are the supported frontend events.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests were provided for the docs routes or for rendered documentation content, so contradictory event names can be introduced without a failing check.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a docs contract test that renders or statically scans SIPIntegration and Notifications and asserts the documented Socket.IO call event names come from a single shared list.
minimumFixScope: Update the Socket.IO Events block in src/pages/docs/sections/SIPIntegration.jsx.

history.reasoning: The original evidence paths still exist. Notifications.jsx still documents the canonical events at current lines 237, 252, and 262: 'notification', 'call-event', and 'incoming-call'. SIPIntegration.jsx no longer contains the reported namespaced events; its real-time section now documents socket.on('call-event', ...) at current lines 212 and 219, with the specific call state carried in data.event. A repository-wide src search found no remaining 'call:incoming', 'call:answered', 'call:ended', or 'call:missed' references, and confirmed runtime/frontend usage of the canonical 'call-event' and 'incoming-call' names in src/hooks/useSocket.js and src/routes/calls.js. No test script exists in package.json; the attempted lint run was rejected by the read-only sandbox policy, so validation is based on static inspection and current code search.
